evidence_quotes.quote: the practice of destroying the ability for consumers to access their purchased games after official support has ended
end-user license agreements which allow publishers to revoke access to product without breaking terms of service

paragraphs.text: I believe it would be wise to add to this act the protection of digital media. More specifically, within the video-game industry, the practice of destroying the ability for consumers to access their purchased games after official support has ended, has become common place. This manifests in end-user license agreements which allow publishers to revoke access to product without breaking terms of service, leaving purchased products in unusable states. I believe these practices clearly take advantage of the lack of regulation in this regard and that is something that should be changed.
